resources.string: Allows users to be notified if a door has been held open too long. The doors that may be notified on are determined by the
A door being held open too long is determined by the

options of the door. After the Held time expires, a Warning phase begins to alert nearby users about the open door, often via buzzers. This warning phase allows users to close the door before the issue escalates, possibly alerting a monitoring station about the issue or sending notifications.
- No user access events will be sent.
- A notification will be sent when the Held Warning phase begins for a door. This could allow a user who should be nearby to close the door before the event gets escalated.

- A notification will be sent when the door has been held open too long and no one has closed it. This event is sent at the end of the Warning phase, or straight away if no Warning phase is configured.
- A notification will be sent when the Held Warning phase begins and ends.

string.name: 95D755E4BCDAC248FD320186E9BC8920
string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "AccessEvents" entries

resources.string: All Access Events
Allows users to be notified about user access attempts. The doors that may be notified on are determined by the
- Any user access attempts that are successfully granted access through the door will be notified. The message will include which door was accessed and who accessed it

- Any user access attempts that are denied access through the door will be notified. The message will include which door the attempt was made at, who made the request and why the request failed (either due to the door being locked down, the area on the other side of the door being armed or no permissions)
- Both successful and failed door access attempts will be notified
Anti-Passback Violations
Allows users to be notified if a user has triggered an anti-passback violation at a door. The doors that may be notified on are determined by the
The event includes information about the user who caused the violation, the door it occured on, the area they were entering and the user's last known location.
Anti-passback logic must be enabled on the door for violations to occur. See the
section on the
[Configuration > Access Control > Door]

string.name: F60A3C93D19B8B4545EA60B506129E02
string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "ScheduledDoorEvents" entries

resources.string: Free Access
Scheduled Events

string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "DoorOverrides" entries

resources.string: Just Door Lockouts

string.name: 6C37CBDA8237A098B8B2A811927B1D3C
string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "DoorOverrides" entries

resources.string: All Override Events
Door State Overrides

Allows users to be notified when a door's state has been overridden by a user or the system. This involves, for example, overriding a door to be unlocked when it is normally locked, or locked when it is normally unlocked. It also includes locking out a door, preventing any user access attempts from working. The doors that may be notified on are determined by the
- No door override events will be sent.

- Users will be notified when the door is set into the Locked Out state. This state prevents any users from being allowed access through the door, allowing the door to be locked down if necessary. Any user access attempts that are successfully granted access through the door will be notified. The message will include which door was locked out and who locked it out

- Users will be notified of all door override events. This includes overriding the door state to Locked, Unlocked and Locked Out, as well as clearing of an override to return it to normal behaviour. The message will include which door was overridden, how it was overridden and who controlled it
Inactive Credential Usage
If enabled, allows users to be notified if a user badges an inactive (i.e. lost/stolen) credential at a door reader.
The event includes information about the credential used, the user associated with the credential, and the door/reader at which the credential was badged.
Hardware Notifications
Allows users to be notified if any module goes offline or comes back online.
A module is considered offline if it fails to contact the controller within its configured
Poll Time
, and remains offline until the
Module Failed Time
expires (configurable on the
page).
Power and Battery Issues
Allows users to be notified about any power or battery issues related to any module in the system. Notification messages will be sent both when the issue occurs and when it is restored.
Module power and battery issues include any of the following:

string.name: B8EB979FA01DAE0651F5BACB17887168
string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "ModuleTroubleType" entries

resources.string: AC Fail
Short Detected
System Tampers
Allows users to be notified about any system tamper events on any module in the system. Notification messages will be sent both when the issue occurs and when it is restored.
System tampers are often indicators that the system could be compromised. System tamper events include any of the following:
Allows users to be notified if any module is detected as being potentially substituted.

During commissioning, when all hardware modules are configured and online, a LAN secure process is performed to identify all present modules. After that, if a module connects that was not online when the LAN secure process was performed, they are considered to be substituted. This could be part of maintenance such as replacing a damaged module, or could be compromising the site security by replacing an existing module with a fake version.
If a module substitution error occurs, a technician is needed to inspect the hardware and LAN to ensure the security of the system before performing a new LAN secure process.
LCD Terminal PIN Lockout
Allows users to be notified if an LCD Terminal triggers its PIN Lockout mode. This could indicate that someone is attempting to guess a user's PIN to gain access to the system.
PIN Lockout is triggered when multiple failed PIN attempts occur at an LCD terminal in a row. The number of attempts required to trigger PIN lockout is configured on a per-terminal basis with the
Max Login Attempts
Module Health Isolations

Allows users to be notified when any health issues related to a module are overridden or isolated. Overriding of health issues causes the system to temporarily stop monitoring the module, often allowing areas to be armed.

Temporary isolation occurs if the health issue was present when an area was armed, allowing the arm to succeed. This isolation will last until the area is disarmed. Users can also permenantly isolate modules from reporting issues if there is an ongoing fault with the module, removing the need to temporarily isolate the health issue each time the area is armed.
System Notifications

Allows users to be notified if the Inception system resets. The notification will include the reason for the reset if it can be detected, such as power loss, firmware update, database restore and several others.
Allows users to be notified if a User Duress event occurs.
User duress events can be triggered using a number of options:
RF Remote Fob
- A single button RF Fob can be configured for User Duress mode, triggering user duress when the button is pressed
Terminal Logged Off Duress
- If enabled (by enabling the
User Duress Reporting
page), a user duress event can be triggered by pressing the
HELP
button on the terminal three times in a row while the terminal is logged off
System Duress User
- If enabled (in the
section of the
page), a user duress event is triggered if the duress user PIN is entered on an LCD Terminal or a keypad reader.
The message will include the name of the user if it is known or the module where the event was triggered.
RF Remote Low Battery
Allows users to be notified when an RF Remote assigned to a user reports a low battery event. The notification will include the name of the user who owns the remote.
Low battery events are only available from Inovonics remotes.
Web Interface Login
Allows users to be notified when a user successfully logs into the Inception web interface. The notification will include the name of the user who logged on.
Web Interface Lockout

Allows users to be notified if Inception triggers lockout mode on the web interface. This could indicate that someone is attempting to guess a user's web login credentials to gain access to the system.

Web interface lockout is triggered when multiple failed web login attempts occur in a row. The number of attempts required to trigger web interface lockout is three, preventing any users from logging in for a short period of time. If the failed login attempts continue, the amount of time that the web interface is locked out for will increase, reducing the effectiveness of a brute-force attack.
Service Mode Change
Allows users to be notified whenever service mode is enabled or disabled in the system. The message will include which user enabled or disabled service mode.

Service mode allows features like sirens and alarm reporting to be temporarily disabled to allow maintenance of the system without having to modify programming, however if done without permission it could compromise the security of the system.
Alarm Reporting Failure
Allows users to be notified if an alarm reporting path to an alarm monitoring station has broken or has been restored.

Reporting path failures are typically also known by the monitoring station, as the reporting paths are polled. Any alarms that occur while the path is down will be queued up to be sent once the connection restores itself.

string.name: 54F71C3F6F025990F3B8CD10361A5E96

resources.string: System Settings

string.name: 76D8F3C00672F61B39846B2112D356A8
string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "EntityChangeNotifications" entries

resources.string: Notification Settings
Item Changes

Allows users to be notified whenever a programming change is made in the system. The message will include which user made the change, the item that was changed and whether the item was created, updated or deleted.
Multiple item types can be specified for the notifier to monitor, allowing users to be informed when settings that shouldn't be touched are modified.

If enabled, the output will become a permissible item. You can assign it directly to a user or add it to the user’s permission group. Once assigned to a user, the user will be able to control the output from the web interface via the
[Dashboard]
[Configuration > Outputs]
[State / Control > Control Outputs]
pages. The user can also control and output via the LCD terminal by logging in and navigating to
[MENU]
Logged Off Controllable
If enabled, the output can be controlled from the LCD terminal without needing a user to log on.
Pressing the
[UP]
key on the terminal will display the outputs, and the
[ON]
[OFF]
buttons will control the outputs.
This option tries to strike a balance between security and convenience, it is not recommended to enable this option for outputs only certain people should be allowed to control.
You can restrict the terminals that provide logged off output control by disabling the
Logged Off Output Control
option, allowing only certain terminals to offer this functionality.
Hardware Output
Informational only: this is the physical location where the output is connected to in the Inception system.
Invert Output State
If enabled, the physical hardware output would be in the opposite state to the one shown in the system.
This is useful when using the
Normally Closed
contacts of a relay output, allowing the output to be turned
in the system and show that state, but have the physical output be closed.
Custom Output
Permission Group

Permissions determine what areas, doors and outputs a user can access or control on the system. The permissions also determine what items the user can control via the web interface. Permissions are created using a
structure. The
determines what the permission allows them to control and in what way, such as accessing a door or disarming an area. The
determines when this permission applies, such as
, or based on a time period, area state or door state for example.
To add permissions, click the
button, this will open a window where you will select the permissible items. If this list is empty, the programming of doors, areas and outputs has not been completed.
For a user to control an output, it must be configured as a user controllable output in the
page, otherwise it will not appear in the add items list.
Each permission can be marked as
. A user must be assigned an
permission to be allowed to do something. If set to
, then when the
condition is true, the user will be unable to perform the action listed in the
column. This
permission will override all other

permissions, that is, even if allowed the same permission in other places the user would still be denied. This is used when combining permission groups together. For example, if you want this permission group to include the same permissions as one or more other permission groups, with an exception or two. Those exceptions could then be added as a
row.
System State
Process Group
Process Groups

Informational only: the door that this reader is linked to. It is possible to change the door via the
Informational only: the side of the door where this reader is located. It is possible to change the location via the
3 Badge Action
This determines the action that occurs when a user badges a card three times at the reader.
- no action will occur.
Arm Area on Other Side of Door
- this will arm the area that is located on the opposite side of the door to where this reader is located, this area is configured in the door settings on the
page. The arm request will fail if the user does not have permission to arm the area, or if there are unsealed inputs in that area.

string.name: 18200094894522E252E692B48F034C2E
string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "AreaToArm" entries

resources.string: Arm All Areas

- this will arm every area in the system that the user has permission to arm. The arm request will fail if the user does not have permission to arm any areas, or one of the areas they can control contains unsealed inputs.

If an arm request fails, the user can attempt to find which input was unsealed and fix it, or try to arm via an LCD Terminal or the web interface which will provide additional information for why the arm failed.
The SIFER/OSDP reader will provide a confirmation tone on successful arming and disarming of areas via the 3 badge action. Additional feedback for area arming can be programmed in the
[Configuration > Areas > Edit Area]
Reader Type
Informational only: it is possible to change the reader type via the
Reader Programming
Disable Reader Lockout
If set, the reader lockout functionality for this reader will be disabled.

The reader lockout feature helps to protect the system from brute force attacks where multiple access cards or PIN numbers are attempted in order to impersonate a user. If multiple access cards or PIN credentials are presented at a reader that do not match a user, reader lockout is triggered. Only credentials that do not match a user are counted, attempts that are denied due to permissions do not count.

Three failed credential attempts in a row trigger a lockout on this reader which will last for 90 seconds. Once triggered, any credentials presented to the reader will be ignored until the lockout time expires. After the lockout time has expired, repeat invalid credentials will cause the 90 second lockout time to restart.

Editing and saving of a User within the system resets the invalid credential count for all readers and clears reader lockout if it has been triggered. This allows cards to be assigned to users by swiping an unknown card at a reader, then assigning that unknown card swipe to a user, without worrying about triggering reader lockout.
Informational only: this is the module that the reader is connected to.

Keypad Type
Allows the selection of the the type of Wiegand keypad. The type selected will determine how information about PIN key presses are interpreted.
Keypad Area To Control
bottom right
key on the keypad to be used to control an area's state (often the # key or the ON/OFF key on an Inner Range Weatherproof keypad.
If set to

, the key will function as normal, requesting access to the door for the PIN that was entered. Otherwise, the key will use the PIN entered to try to control the area instead of requesting access to the door. How the area is controlled will depend on the current state of the area. If the area is armed (including when entry or exit delay is active), an alarm is currently active or the area is about to arm, the system will attempt to disarm the area. Otherwise, the system will attempt to arm the area. The user who entered the PIN must also have the correct permissions for these actions to succeed.

resources.string: Light Yellow
Provide Door State Feedback
If enabled, the SIFER/OSDP reader will provide feedback for the following states:
DOTL
- If the door is held open for too long the reader will make a beeping sound until the door reed or tongue sense are sealed. DOTL functionality is configured in the
[Configuration > Access Control > Doors]
- When a user without permission to access the door badges a card, the SIFER reader will make a single beep and the Big LED will flash red.
- When the door is unlocked by any method the SIFER reader will make a single beep and the Big LED will flash green.
Provide Area (Same Side) State Feedback
If enabled, the SIFER/OSDP reader will provide feedback for the area located on the same side of the door as the reader. Feedback is provided for the following area states:
Area Arm Success
- When the area is successfully armed, the SIFER reader will sound the Area Arm Success tone and the Big LED will flash green.
Area Arm Failure
- When the area fails to arm, either due to permissions or unsealed inputs, the SIFER reader will sound the Area Arm Failure tone and the Big LED will flash red.
Area Active Alarm
- When there is an active alarm in the area, the SIFER reader will sound the Area Alarm tone and the Small LED will fast flash red until the area is disarmed.
Entry Delay Active
- When the area's entry delay mode is active, the SIFER reader will sound the Entry Delay tone and the Small LED will flash green for the duration of the entry delay.
Exit Delay Active
- When the area's exit delay mode is active, the SIFER reader will sound the Exit Delay tone and the Small LED will flash green for the duration of the exit delay.
Area About to Arm

- When the area is about to automatically arm, the SIFER reader will sound the Area Auto Arm Warning tone and the Small LED will flash orange until the area is automatically armed or manually disarmed.

Note: The states above are prioritised by the system in the order they are listed. If the SIFER reader is configured to provide area state feedback for the areas on both sides of the door, both areas are tested and the area state with the higher priority will be provided.
Provide Area (Other Side) State Feedback
If enabled, the SIFER/OSDP reader will provide feedback for the area located on the other side of the door to the reader. Feedback is provided for the following area states:
Status LED to Follow

This determines the purpose of the reader's status LED. For SIFER readers, this is the small LED, for other OSDP readers, this is the only LED. This LED can be used to display a static colour or the door or area state.
- the status LED will be the colour selected in the LED colour field on this page.

- the status LED will turn red if the door is locked and will turn green when the door is unlocked. The status LED will also flash red if the user badges a card and is denied access to the door. Selecting this option will override the
Small LED Colour
field for SIFER readers, or the
LED Colour
field for OSDP readers on this page.
Area state, same side & other side

- the status LED will turn red if the area is armed and will turn green when the area is disarmed. This can be used to display the state of the area on either the same side as the reader or the opposite side. The status LED will flash green when a user is granted access to the door and will flash red if the user is denied access to the door. Selecting this option will override the
key ([#] key) on the SIFER/OSDP keypad to be used to control an area's state.
Reader Serial Number
Informational only: it is possible to change hardware assigned via the
page. This is the identification number of the SIFER/IR reader, this number is printed on the rear of the reader.
Volume
Sets the volume of the tones generated by the SIFER reader. There are numerous actions that will generate a beeping tone such as card access, area control etc.
Brightness
Sets the brightness of the SIFER readers big and small led.
Big LED Colour
Sets the colour of the big LED.
Sets the colour of the small LED. Only applies if the above
option is set to
Reader Address
page. This is the OSDP Bus address of the OSDP reader, and is what the reader uses to identify itself with the module.
Sets the colour of the LED.
EAC Number
The calculated EAC number of the Aperio wireless lock.
This EAC can be found within the Aperio Programming Application, assigned by the lock's parent hub to the lock based off the hub's own EAC and the lock number.
SIFER Readers
OSDP Reader
OSDP Readers

resources.string: Date Range
Report Config
Report Parameters
Report Type
The type of report to generate.

- Generates a report which shows a list of events where Users have requested access through Doors or access to Lift Cars, including the time of the access event and whether the access was granted or denied. Optionally filterable by Users, Doors or Lift Cars, and Access Granted/Denied Status.

- Generates a report which shows a list of events where Users or the System have requested to arm or disarm Areas. Optionally filterable by Areas, Users, Arm/Disarm events, User/System events, and Successful/Failed events.

- Generates a report which shows the list of item permissions and Permission Groups that each User has been assigned. Has the option of generating different levels of detail for reports, depending on how much information is required. Optionally filterable by Users and Items.

- Generates a report which shows the list of all Users that have permission to interact with each item. Similar to the User Permissions Summary, except the report is grouped by the item. Optionally filterable by Users and Items.
- Generates a report which shows a list of Users along with the Area that they are currently in. Optionally filterable by Areas.
- Generates a report which shows all Users who have not interacted with the system (i.e. accessed a Door, armed an Area) for a given period of time (default is 1 month).

- Generates a report containing detailed summaries of Users in the system. The details shown for each user include (if applicable) the User's Name, Notes, LCD Terminal Profile, Email Address, Assigned Cards & Remote Fobs, and more. Optionally filterable by Users, and Users who are Expired/Expiring Soon.

- Generates a report which shows a list of time periods that Users have been on site for, based on access events from the specified Read-In/Read-Out Readers. Optionally filterable by Users. This type of report is recommended if the site has strict Badge-in, Badge-out policies when entering and leaving site. Report will show the amount of time between each badge in and each badge out, listing per-user each site visit over the report timeframe. Read-In and Read-Out readers must be specified.

- Generates a report which shows a list of Users have been on site each day and for how long, based on their first and last reader badge events for the day (a single day is treated as being from 12am-11:59pm). Optionally filterable by Users and Doors. This type of report is recommended if Users are only on site during regular daytime hours (i.e. no night shifts going past midnight), and if your site don't have strictly defined in/out readers.

- Generates a report which shows a list of events where Users or the System have requested to secure or unlock Storage Units. Optionally filterable by Storage Units, Users, Secure/Unlock events, User/System events, Successful/Failed events, and whether Vacancy Control events should be included.
- Generates a report which shows a list of events raised by an Integrated Connection or its Integrated Devices. Optionally filterable by Integrated Devices, as well as specific event types.
File Format
Chooses the file format of the report.

- Generates the report as a single PDF document. NOTE: This option may be unable to handle creating very large reports (e.g. User Permissions Summary Reports of 500+ pages) due to system limitations; add filters to the data to reduce the report size or choose one of the other file formats if you need to create a very large report.
- Generates the report as a single HTML document.
- Generates the report as a series of individual PDF documents of approximately 150 pages each at maximum, packed together for downloading in a single ZIP file.
CSV

- Generates the report as a comma-separated value (CSV) text file, ideal for manipulation or processing in external applications, e.g. spreadsheet software. NOTE: CSV reports are text-only and therefore lack the formatting of the other file type options; if the report's readability is important, consider using one of the PDF or HTML format options instead.
Report Name
Generate a report which user have requested with Title Text as in Format "Report Name (Report Type)".User can provide name for Report in Report Name field.
By default Report Name would be Report Type and this case Title Text would be in "Report Type".
Report Timeframe Type
The type of timeframe that will be used to fetch events to be included in the generated report.

- Allows you to specify an amount of time from the present to fetch events to be put into the generated report. For example, picking 2 Weeks as the Timeframe for this option gets the last 2 Weeks of events in the report.
- Allows you to specify a Start and End Date which defines the time period to fetch events from.
Specifies the start date for finding events that will be included in the generated report.
Specifies the end date for finding events that will be included in the generated report.
Timeframe
Specifies how far back in time in time to go when generating the report.
Filter To Specific Areas
Optionally filter the report results to only include users that are currently in the specified Areas. By default (when the list is empty), all Areas are included in the report.
Exclude Users not in Areas

Specifies whether to exclude Users from the report if the system does not currently know what Area they are in (i.e. inactive users). NOTE: Only applies when the report is not filtered to specific Areas.
Filter To Specific Users
Optionally filter the report results to only include the selected Users. By default (when the list is empty), all Users are included in the report.
Filter To Specific Items
Optionally filter the report results to only include the selected Items. By default (when the list is empty), all Items (Doors, Areas, Outputs) are included in the report.

resources.string: Fully Flattened Permissions (Standard)

string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "UserPermissionsReportFormat" entries

resources.string: Fully Flattened Permissions + Group Chains (Advanced)

string.name: F674B6C25192A2801A0A4BC56F571DC7
string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "UserPermissionsReportFormat" entries

resources.string: Directly Assigned Permissions Only (Basic)
Report Level of Detail
Chooses the level of detail to be included in the User Permissions Report.

- The report shows a flattened view of all the items a user has access to through both direct assignment and Permission Groups, and also the conditions which determine when these permissions are available (e.g. Always, only during Working Hours). This type of report is best suited for getting a clear view of all the possible actions that Users can perform in the system.

- The report contains all of the information in Fully Flattened Permissions (Standard), as well as a description of why these permissions are available to the User (e.g. directly assigned, inherited from a Permission Group). This type of report is best suited for getting a highly detailed, in-depth look at all of the possible actions that Users can perform in the system, as well as the reason(s) why these permissions are available to them.
Directly Assigned Permissions only (Basic)

- The report shows all of the directly assigned permissions for each user, including the Permission Groups they are assigned to. This report does not flatten out Permission Groups and shows only the direct assignments. This type of report is best suited for getting a simple overview of what items each User has been directly assigned permission to in the system, or seeing which Users have been assigned to which Permission Groups.

Expires Within
option controls the time period to search.

resources.string: Create Report
Specifies the type of action that this Scheduled Task should perform when it is executed.
- Creates a database backup file containing the database contents as they are at the time the scheduled task runs.
- Creates a report with the specified parameters given. Allows creation of any type of report that can be created from the Reports page.

resources.string: Copy to Network Share
Specifies how the scheduled task should send out the resulting output file after it is produced.
- Send the output file to one or more Users via their email address.
- Writes the output file to the root folder of an attached USB drive, if one is currently plugged into the Inception Device and accessible.
- Copies the output file to a Samba network share folder using the provided user/domain credentials.
Users to Email

Specifies the list of Users to send the output file to via email. If there are no users specified, or all specified Users have an invalid or missing email address, the task will fail to send the output file.
Network Share Folder Path
Specifies the network share folder to copy the output file to, e.g:
//example-server/shared
NOTE: The folder specified in the network path must already exist in order for the operation to complete successfully.
Specifies the username to use for network/domain authentication. If on a domain network, the domain may also need to be specified before a / character, e.g
domain/username
Specifies the user's password to use for network/domain authentication.

string.name: 9B6545E4CEA9B4AD4979D41BB9170E2B
string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "ScheduledTaskRecurringTimeType" entries

resources.string: Advanced
Schedule Frequency
Specifies how often this Scheduled Task should be automatically run.

- This task will not run at regular intervals. Use this option if you plan to execute this task either manually or only when certain conditions occur, e.g. triggering it as part of an Automated Action.
- This task will be run at set intervals of uniform length, e.g. Daily, Weekly, Monthly, at a specific time of day.
- Utilises the Calendar items in Inception (configured via the
[Configuration > Scheduling > Calendars]

page). This task will run on a set of days specified by a separate Calendar item, at a specific time of day. Multiple Calendar/Time of Day combinations can be specified to make the task run at additional times.

resources.string: Every 4 Weeks
Frequency
Specifies the interval that determines how long to wait in between task executions.
- The task will run every day at the specfied
Time of Day
- The task will run every week on the specified
Day of Week
, at the specfied
- The task will run every 2 weeks on the specified
, starting on the closest future applicable day/time at the time the Scheduled Task item is saved.
- The task will run every 4 weeks on the specified
Specifies the day of the week on which this task should be run, in accordance with the rest of the schedule configuration settings.
Not applicable if
is chosen as the frequency.
Specifies the time of day on which this task should be run, in accordance with the rest of the schedule configuration settings.
Calendar Times
Specifies the Calendar items and times at which the scheduled task should run. The scheduled task will run at the time specified, on the days provided by the calendar.

Each row defines a different schedule entry, for example, you could define 2 Calendar items to represent Weekends and Public Holidays respectively, and set the task to run at 2:00pm on Weekends and 5:00pm on Public Holidays, and the task will run on every day that either of the relevant Calendars are active for, at their respective scheduled time.

resources.string: Serial Port {0}
Unplugged
In Use By
Not In Use
Entry Readers

Specifies the readers that can control the entry points to this storage block that allows a user to present either their PIN, card, or other credential. Multiple readers can be assigned to the same storage block if required, allowing readers to be mounted in different locations or be of different types.

The selected readers can also be connected to other items such as doors or gates, allowing the User's access request to an entry gate or a building to also be used to control the units within the storage block.
On successful read of a User's credential, the system will unlock any units within the block that the user has
Control on Block Access
permission to. This allows a User's access to site to automatically unlock their unit.
To add a reader to the storage block, click the
Exit Readers

Specifies the readers that can control the exit points to this storage block that allows a user to present either their PIN, card, or other credential. Multiple readers can be assigned to the same storage block if required, allowing readers to be mounted in different locations or be of different types.

The selected readers can also be connected to other items such as doors or gates, allowing the User's access request to an exit gate or to exit a building to also be used to control the units within the storage block.
On successful read of a User's credential, the system will secure any units within the block that the user has
permission to. This allows a User's request to exit a building or the site to automatically secure their unit as they leave.
Block Outputs

Allows one or more outputs to be associated with this storage block. If any units in the block are unlocked,secured or in alarm state, the outputs in this list will behave according to output behavior. If output behavior of an output is lighting, this will turn on output when unit is unblocked. If output behavior of an output is Secure Indicator, this will turn on output when unit is secure. If output behavior of an output is Alarm Indicator, this will turn on output when unit is in Alarm state.

For example, these outputs could be used to control lighting, secure indicator and alarm indicator within a section of the building, turning on if any units in that section are unlocked, secure and in alarm state.
To add outputs to the storage block, click the
button, this will open a window where you select the outputs to add. If there are no outputs in this window, new outputs can be configured by navigating to the
Output Off Delay Time
Optionally defines a delay before the
are turned off. When set to
, these outputs will turn off immediately. When set to any other value, the outputs will wait this amount of time before turning off.
This is useful for scenarios where the final unit was secured at a keypad inside the facility, and some time is required before the interior lights are turned off.
Block Units
Unit Auto-Resecure Time
If configured, units within this storage block will automatically re-secure after a period of time.
This helps ensure that a unit will automatically re-secure after a while and not be left in an unlocked state.
As this option requires knowledge of a unit's door state, the unit must have at least one input with the

string.name: D06263308C256251EC14D975A36E0619
string.notes: Must be unique amongst all "UnitInputType" entries

resources.string: Door Position Sensor
behaviour.
is chosen, the resecure logic does not occur.
If a unit is part of more than one block, the shortest time that is not
is chosen for that unit.
Auto-Resecure Timer Trigger
If configured, units within this storage block will enter an
state if the unit remains unlocked for the specified time. This state is cancelled once the unit is secured or if marked as vacant.
This helps identify if a unit has been unintentionally left unlocked for a long period of time. It also helps identify if users are intentionally unlocking and staying in a unit.
Force Secure Units with Active Inputs
Vacancy Clear with Active Inputs Action
Clear Vacancy on Valid Card Read
This setting determines the response to the sealing of all inputs of a storage unit that is in Alarm.
By leaving this option disabled, the unit will remain in Alarm until a

action occurs for the unit, latching the alarm state. Clearing the alarm could be by the units tenant presenting their credential at the Block entry reader to unlock it, or by a User controlling the unit via the
[State / Control > Control Storage Units]
page on the Inception web interface. This allows users to easily see which unit triggered the alarm, and allows monitoring stations to know when the alarm event has actually been restored.

If the option is enabled, the Unit alarm state will be cleared as soon as all inputs within the secure unit are sealed or when the unit is unlocked. This allows the unit alarm state to follow the state of its inputs, but has the consequence that if a unit only has a door sensor then someone could open a secure unit door, enter, then close it straight away, and the unit would no longer be in alarm.
For unmanned storage unit facilities, it is recommended that this option is disabled and that the area configured to monitor the storage units alarm states has the
Cancel Alarm on All Inputs Secure

option enabled. This will allow sirens and alarms to occur if someone accesses a secure storage unit, but if it was an accident, then the units tenant can unlock the unit and the alarm can be cancelled.
Turn on Lighting Outputs When Unit is Vacant
Outputs with the Lighting behaviour will be turn on when the unit is vacant if Turn on Lighting Outputs When Unit is Vacant is enabled
Allows the units that belong to this block to be selected. Access requests at the
specified above can automatically unlock units within this list, and the
will be controlled based on the unit states.
The status of these units are summarized for this block, indicating if all units are secure, or if any are unlocked, unlocked too long, in alarm, or have been tampered with.
To add a unit to the storage block, click the
button, this will open a window where you select the units to add. If there are no units in this window, new units can be configured by navigating to the
[Configuration > Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units]
Unit Inputs
Specifies the inputs that are used to monitor this Unit. Each input can have a Behaviour specified, which determines how that input's state affects the Unit.

- An input with this behaviour lets the system know if the Unit's door is currently opened or closed. In addition, if the Unit is Secure, activation of this input will put the Unit into the Alarm state.
- An input with this behaviour will cause a Secure Unit to enter the alarm state when it activates
If an input is tampered with, the unit will enter into a Tamper state.
To add inputs to the Unit, click the
button, this will open a window where you select the inputs to add. If there are no inputs in this window, new inputs can be configured by navigating to the
Unit Outputs
Allows one or more outputs to be associated with this Unit. If the unit is Unlocked, the outputs in this list will turn on, and when the Unit is Secure, they will turn off.
For example, these outputs could be used to control a light within the unit or the Unit's door lock. They could also be used as a visual indicator if the unit is Secure (if wired to the
contacts of the relay, or if the
option is enabled for that output on the
[Configuration > Outputs > Hardware Outputs]
To add outputs to the Unit, click the

resources.string: Inner Range Mobile Credentials
Start Time
End Time
All Day (24hr)
Time Period Active
Priority
Select calendar exceptions to apply to this Time Period.
These exceptions are days/times in which this time period will be inactive. These are defined in the Calendars menu.
Weekly Recurring Times
Defines the start and ends times of the time period, for each day of the week. During these times and on the days selected the time period will be valid/active.

If the start time is earlier than the end time (e.g 9am -> 5pm), then the time period will be valid during those times on the day specified. Alternatively, if the start and end time are the same (5pm -> 5pm) or the end time is earlier than the start (10pm -> 2am), then the valid block will start on the day specified and end on the next day. This allows an easy way to specify time blocks spanning midnight. This means, for exmaple, 10pm -> 2am on a Wednesday will be valid from 10pm to midnight on Wednesdays, and valid from midnight to 2am on Thursdays. Similarly, if the start and end times match, 5pm -> 5pm on Wednesday will be valid for 24 hours from 5pm Wednesday to 5pm Thursday.
For the time period to be valid for an entire day, 12am -> 12am can be configured, or the
can be selected.
To add additional time blocks, click the
button, this will open a window where the times and days can be selected.
Times
Calendar Exceptions

A list of exceptions which apply on the days when their associated calendar is active (higher priorities will override lower ones). A time period that contains calendar exceptions will have its active or inactive state overridden by the calendar exception on the days for which it’s configured.

Calendar exceptions are used when the same time change for a day should be repeated on multiple days throughout the year. The calendar can be updated, which could affect multiple Time Periods in a consistent way. A common use case example for calendar exceptions are extended trading hour days, where trading hours change consistently on many days throughout the year. The calendar in that case could apply to multiple time periods, like working hours, automatic door times and automatic arm times, each reacting to the same calendar. A user can then update the calendar throughout the year and the system reacts correctly.
Start and end times for the exception are programmed in the same way as the

option above, where a start time that is earlier than the end time (e.g 9am -> 5pm) affects a single day, but the same start and end time (5pm -> 5pm) or an earlier end time (10pm -> 2am) will span midnight and affect the following day too. More details can be seen in the tooltip of the
option above.
To add a calendar exception, click the
button, this will open a window that contains all the available calendars. If there are no calendars, they can be created in the
Exceptions

A list of individual time and date based exceptions which override the list of weekly recurring times (higher priorities will override lower ones). Exceptions behave in the same manner as calendar exceptions, overriding the active or inactive state of the time period's weekly recurring times, however these exceptions only exist within this particular time period.

Commonly used to add a temporary specific change to the time period's normal behaviour without changing the recurring times, which could also be added in advance. For example, shutting down for a maintenance period or a brief period of extended trading hours.
To add an additional exception, click the
button, this will add a blank exception.
Security PIN
The security PIN is a unique code that allows a user to control areas and outputs from an LCD terminal, or access a door from a Wiegand keypad reader.

The security PIN can also be used to log onto the web interface instead of using a password. The security PIN will not be visible once the user is saved, if the user has forgotten their security PIN, you may press the “Send PIN” button. This will open a new window which allows an email with the Security PIN and basic details to be sent to the user. Otherwise, another user with higher privileges (such as an installer) with “Reveal PIN” permissions may use this button to reveal and view the PIN.
Security PINs must be unique across users, you will be unable to save changes to a user if a duplicate pin is entered. To generate a unique pin for a user click the
Auto-Generate PIN

button, this will open a window containing the new PIN. The default requirements for the security PIN length is a minimum of 2 digits and a maximum of 4 digits. If you enter a pin outside of these requirements, you will be unable to save the changes. These PIN length limits can be changed by an administrator by navigating to
. Note, if increasing PIN length limits, LCD Terminals can accept at most 8 digit PINs and the
is limited to at most 10 digits.

A credential is a uniquely numbered card, fob, or other type of identifiable data (e.g. a biometric fingerprint or face scan). The credential number is read by a reader at a door to identify which user is attempting to access the door.

A credential can only be assigned to a single user, if a duplicate credential is assigned you will be unable to save the user. Users may be assigned multiple credentials if, for example, your site uses more than one type of card or biometric reader technology.
To add a credential to a user, click the
button, this will open a window where you select the credential template. If there are no templates in this window, a template can be configured by navigating to
[Configuration > Access Control > Credential Templates]
Once a template is selected, enter the number that is printed on the card (or other credential type). If there is no number on the card, you can identify the credential number by navigating to
and then badging the credential on the reader, find the most recent
Credential Read Failed Because Credential is Unassigned
event, this event will contain the credential number.
Credentials also contain a state field (which defaults to Active) which determines whether the credential will be accepted at door readers or not. Door readers will only accept credentials marked as
, credentials marked as
Inactive - Lost
Inactive - Stolen
for example, will be rejected, and a review event and system message about the inactive credential usage will be logged. All credentials marked as
are handled the same way by the system, the extra information such as
Stolen

is provided only for informational purposes. This functionality can be useful to disable the use of a credential in the event that a user's credential is reported as lost/stolen, without having to delete the credential entry from the User's data.
Mobile Credentials are uniquely numbered cards used as identification for this user that is handled by a Mobile Access system. This requires a Mobile Access system to be linked through
[Configuration > Access Control > Mobile Access > Mobile Access Connection Settings]

When the system has been linked, the Allocate Mobile Credential button will be usable. This will allocate a Mobile Credential to the user, with an email invitation sent to the specified email. The invitation can be accepted on their phone with the Mobile Access application. That user may only have one Mobile Credential from any given system.
The system uses a preconfigured Mobile Access Credential Template that can be found in
. The site code of this template will be the site code specified when linking the Mobile Access system.
A remote fob is a wireless 1 or 4 button keychain remote that is used to remotely control areas, doors, outputs etc. The 1 button remote can also be used as a mobile duress pendant.
A remote fob can only to be assigned to a single user, if a duplicate fob is assigned you will be unable to save the user.
To add a remote fob to a user, click the

button, this will open a window where you select the fob template. The fob template determines the functionality for the buttons on the remote. If there are no templates in this window, a template can be configured by navigating to
[Configuration > Access Control > Remote Fob Templates]
Once a template is selected, enter the six-digit serial number that is printed on the remote fob. If there is no number on the remote, you can identify the serial number by navigating to
and then pressing a button on the remote fob. Find the most recent
Remote Fob Button from Unknown Fob
event, this event will contain the serial number.
The functionality of the remote assigned to the user is based on the programming of the fob template found in
Permissions determine what areas, doors, outputs and lift floors a user can access or control in the system. The permissions also determine what items the user can control via the web interface.
To add permissions to a user, click the
button; this will open a window that contains all the permissible items. These items can be added one at a time or as a pre-defined permission group.
Permission groups are a collection of permissions that are created in the
[Configuration > Users > Permission Groups]

page. Permission groups are created to suit a role of one or more users within a business, for example, warehouse staff or admin staff. This allows the same set of permissions to be assigned to multiple users.
The permissions assigned to a user always apply. More advanced control, such as limiting

a user is allowed to do something, is available within permission groups. This allows more complex permissions that fit in with a sites rules to be created, limiting access based on a time period or something else in the system, while keeping the interface used by end-users simpler.
, then the user will be unable to perform that action.
permissions override

permissions, that is, if someone is allowed through a door due to a permission group, denying access to the door will override this. This can be used when a user should have all of the permissions in a permission group with an exception or two. Those exceptions could then be added as a
row.
The LCD terminal profile determines what menus a user can view when logged into an LCD Terminal. The user will require a
to be able to login to an LCD Terminal. The terminal profile will not affect the logged off functionality of the LCD Terminal such as logged off output control and door control using the OK button.

The profile only determines which menu options the user can view and interact with via the LCD Terminal. If the profile lets the user access the menus that allow area, door or output control, they can see the current state of those items but still need permission to control them in their
below.

Click the dropdown box to select an LCD Terminal Profile. As this is a security and access control system protecting the site, when selecting a profile, it is recommended that you select the one that lets the user do and see everything that they need to, but nothing extra.
The LCD Terminal Profiles are configured on the
[Configuration > Users > Terminal Profiles]
If enabled, when this user is granted access to a door, the door will unlock for the duration programmed in its
, instead of its
. This option is used for users who require extra time to enter a door, like a delivery driver bringing in parcels or a user with a disability or impaired mobility.
The extended lock time can be configured differently for each door and can be programmed in
Permanently Add to SLAM Cache
If enabled, this user's cards will be permantly stored in the internal card caches of any Standard LAN Access Modules (SLAMs) on the module LAN.

The SLAM is capable of holding 2000 cards in an internal cache, tracking whether access was granted the last time it was presented at a reader and remembering this per-door controlled by the SLAM. If communications between the SLAM and the Inception system are lost, the SLAM can use this card cache to determine if a user's card should be allowed through the door, allowing offline behaviour.

Cards are automatically removed from this cache for several reasons; if access is denied to a door when the card is used, after 1 month of inactivity, or if the 2000 limit is reached then as new cards are added to the cache the oldest cards are removed. This attempts to allow an intelligent offline behaviour where the people who use a door recently will still be able to access the door in the event that the door module loses connectivity with the Inception controller.

This option however can force a card to remain stored in the cache, even if it hasn't been used for a long time. This allows important cards like those belonging to managers, emergency services or fire wardens etc to remain working if something happens to the main controller, even if they are rarely used.

Note that cards are not automatically added to the card cache of each SLAM, the card must be presented at each reader of each door and access be successfully granted for the card to be added to the cache of that module.
Remote / Web Access
Enable Web Login

If enabled, the user will have access to the web interface using the username and password below. The web interface can be accessed on any device with a supported web browser. The device needs to be on the same local network as the Inception Controller or be connected to the Inception Controllers Wi-Fi Hotspot (requires the optional Inception Wireless Adapter). A user can also remotely connect to the web interface using the Skytunnel service, Skytunnel is a hassle free internet based connection method.
option should only be enabled if the user needs to edit programming, control areas, doors, outputs and inputs or to perform administration tasks from the web interface.

You need to assign a web page profile to a user to determine which pages the user can view and interact with. If the chosen web profile allows control of items such as areas, doors or outputs, the user will only be allowed to control the items that you assign in their user permissions. If the user only has permissions to control an area between working hours the same restriction applies to the control of the area from the web interface.
A login name that is used to identify a user and allow access to the web interface. The username can be any length and any combination of letters, numbers and characters.
Each user requires a unique username, you will be unable to save the user programming if a duplicate username is entered.
A secret series of numbers or letters used with the username to allows access to the web interface. The password can be any length and any combination of letters, numbers and characters.

Since Inception is a security product, it is recommended that this password be kept confidential by the user that it has been assigned to. It is also recommended that the password is not saved by the browser that is used to access the web interface. The Inception Controller's web interface can also be accessed over the internet via the Skytunnel architecture, so it is important to have a secure password that can not be guessed.
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Configuration

The Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) enrolment status of the user. 2FA-enrolled users must enter an extra security code from a personal device before they can log into the web interface. Click the link button to open a dialog window to enrol the user for 2FA. NOTE: 2FA must be enabled in Inception's System Settings in order to enrol users for 2FA.
Once a user has been enrolled for 2FA, they will be prompted to enter a security code generated from their personal device in order to confirm the enrolment was successful.

Pressing the X button will delete the existing 2FA configuration for the user. This will cause the user's existing 2FA accounts on smartphone apps to be rendered obsolete, and will require the user to re-enrol for Inception 2FA in order to use it again.

Once a user has initially enrolled for 2FA, the enrolment dialog will no longer be accessible by clicking the link button for security reasons, the 2FA configuration will have to be deleted and re-created in order to re-enrol.
Not Enrolled
Determines which web pages this user is allowed to see within the web interface and how they are allowed to interact with them; for example view only, modify and control.

Click the dropdown box to select a web page profile. As this is a security and access control system protecting the site, when selecting a profile, it is recommended that you select the one that lets the user do and see everything that they need to, but nothing extra.
Web profiles can be configured on the
[Configuration > Users > Web Page Profiles]

The IR Connect account that is linked to this user. Click the link button to enter the user's IR Connect account (email address). If they don't have an IR Connect account, an email will be automatically sent instructing them how to set up their free account.

Linking an IR Connect account to this user allows the user to control Areas, Doors and Outputs remotely using the IR Connect smartphone app. The app is free to use and only requires that the Inception controller has an active internet connection. Only the items this user has permission to control will be seen in the App. Pressing the X button will unlink this user from the IR Connect account, preventing them from logging in.
The IR Connect account also allows the user to monitor the system remotely by receiving push notification alerts to their smartphone. The notifications sent are configured on the
[Configuration > System > Notifiers]
User API Token
The API Token which is linked to this user, which can be used in the authorization header of REST API requests to make requests on the behalf of this user instead of logging in with another request.
If an API Token is created through the prompt on the link button, it can be copied and added to the authorization header of REST API requests (with type 'APIToken' preceding it).
Unlinking the API Token through the 'X' button will result in the API Token generated for the user being deleted and being rendered unusable.
Personal Details
The user Photo field is an optional field where an image file can be uploaded and associated to a user.
User photos will be displayed with their name in the Who column on the
[Events]
page when events involving this user occur in the system, e.g. Area Arm/Disarm or Door Access events. Users without photos will only have their name displayed in the Who column.

An image file can be assigned to a user by dragging and dropping the file directly onto the image upload area (if using a PC), or by clicking the upload area and selecting the image that you want to upload. The image's dimensions will be scaled down to 480 x 640px (3:4 aspect ratio) if they exceed this size, so it is recommended that uploaded images meet these conditions for optimal quality when uploaded. The image can be cropped using the Edit button, or deleted with the Remove button.
Images can be assigned in bulk to users by using the
Import User Photos
button to upload a ZIP file with multiple image files for different users. For more information, click the
button to open the import dialog.
The email address that is used when sending email notifications to this user.
This option can be left empty if email notifications for this user are not used. Otherwise, the address must be in a valid format such as jsmith@example.com
Expiry / Cancellation
Enable User Expiry
If enabled, this user will not be allowed to access the system before the
User Start Time
if one is provided, or after the
User Expiry Time
if one is provided. If a user is restricted from accessing the system, disabling this option or changing the start or expiry time will re-enable the user again.

User expiry is commonly used for contractors or other users who require access to the system for a limited period of time. It allows their user to be configured in advance with credentials and permissions, but only activate at a future time. Similarly, the user can be automatically restricted to prevent their access at a date and time in the future, removing the need for a user to remember to manually edit or delete the user in the future.

Once expired, this user will not be able to interact with the system in any way, preventing access to the web interface and the LCD terminal, credentials will no longer work at readers, their RF fob will not work and they will no longer receive notifications.
Determines the date and time when this user will become valid. If set in the future, this user will not be allowed to access the system. This option will only apply when the
option above is enabled. If blank, no start time will be used.
See the tooltip for the enable option above for more information about user expiry.
Determines the date and time when this user will expire. This option will only apply when the
option above is enabled. If blank, no expiry will be used.
When to Cancel User
If configured, the user will be cancelled when they present their credential to the system for the first time. Depending on configuration, this could be their card or PIN.

This feature is often used where single-use credentials are given to visitors to access the site, which prevents unlimited access in case the card is stolen. The above user expiry options could also be used to manage visitor cards, allowing access for the day only. This option instead allows allows multiple cards to be pre-created (one per user) and handed out as required, without needing to log into the Inception system and update the expiry time for that day.

Once a credential has been used and the user cancelled, the User Cancelled checkbox below will be automatically ticked. To reset the user and allow the credential to be used again, un-tick the checkbox and save the user.
User Cancelled
If enabled, this user will be restricted from accessing the system in any way, similar to user expiry above. This option is automatically enabled when the above When to Cancel User option is used.
The user can be reset by un-ticking this option and saving the user.
Revoke Mobile Credentials Upon Expiry
If enable, mobile credentials of this user will be revoked upon passing User Expiry time.By default, this option will be unchecked.
Duress Users
Allowed
Add permissions to this user. Permissions can be added to specific doors, areas or outputs or entire permission groups can be added.

Allow User Import/Export

This option can only be enabled if the user has
permissions to the
Edit Users
Allow PIN Reveals

If enabled, allows a user to reveal other user's PINs as long as they have a lower or same priority level. Consider if allowing this user to reveal PINs of lower or same priority level users is appropriate, and if any others should even be allowed to do it (the Installer user has this permission by default). Revealing a user's PIN can be done by pressing the 'Send PIN' button present on the user's management page.

If a user forgets their PIN, they do not require this permission, and can instead either generate a new PIN or use the 'Send PIN' (accessible to anyone) option to send their forgotten PIN to their email address (if they have one).
Allow Password Reveals
If enabled, allows a user to reveal stored passwords of various entities that have a Password field, such as IR Video Gateway Connections, providing they have the
permission to this entity. Revealing a password can be done by pressing the button present on the entity's password field.
Limit User Permission Config to Groups Only
If enabled, users with this profile option will only be allowed to assign permission groups to other users on the
page and will have the individual areas, doors, outputs and so on hidden. By hiding these, the user management process is simplified for end users.

This allows the detailed site permissions to be configured by a technician or a system administrator, while a user responsible for day to day management has a simpler interface. Also, if a site has complex permissions (i.e permission is only allowed during working hours, or advanced door interlocking scenarios etc), it prevents a user from assigning permission to the item directly and circumventing the site rules.
Permission Group Filter
The above
Limit User Permissions Config to Groups Only
option must be enabled to use this filter.

If this list is empty, this user will be able to use any permission group while managing other user's permissions. Adding items to this list restricts the user to only being able to use the specified items when editing permissions.

System messages are informational, telling the user about events that happened in the past such as a system reset or a health issue that has been restored, and just need to be acknowledged to remove. Caution: Permission to execute this task should only be provided to a site administrator or the installer. The user would be able to restore an old backup disabling recently programmed functionality.

Caution: Permission to execute this task should only be provided to the installer. A user with this permission can completely reset the controller disabling all programmed functionality.

Caution: Permission to execute this task should only be provided to the installer. Updating of firmware may include additional tasks that need to be followed which, if not performed correctly, can compromise site security.
Import Custom Items
If enabled, the user can upload custom items provided by Inner Range from the
[Configuration > System]
Note: The user also requires at least View permission on the
Edit System Settings
option in their web page profile to be able to see the
If enabled, the user can place the system into service mode, which will disable alarm reporting and siren responses.
Caution: Permission to execute this task should only be provided to a site administrator or the installer. A user with this permission can compromise site security.
View Cross References
System Permissions
Cross Reference
feature on item editor pages.
Profile Priority Level

A profile's priority level is used to create a user management hierarchy, where users are only allowed to create, edit or delete users who have a profile priority level that is lower or equal to their own. Priority levels range from one to five, with a higher number translating to a higher priority.

This options provides a more secure way of protecting the system, preventing for example the person who is assigned the day to day user management from being able to create a new Installer or Administrator user. Similarly, a more senior user with more permissions and abilities can't be edited by a user with a lower priority level.
Web Page Profiles with Priority Five will be able to edit any Web Page Profile's permissions.
View API Details Page
Determine's a user's ability to view the API Details page under
[Licensing]
Show Technician Shortcuts
Dashboard Content
If enabled, the
page will shows shortcuts to items an installer will generally use, including the commissioning checklist, hardware wizard, alarm reporting and credential templates.
Note: If the user’s web profile does not include the ability to view these items, the respective shortcuts will not appear on the
Show Administrator Shortcuts
page will shows shortcuts to items a system administrator will generally use, including networking, date and time, system overview and database backup/restore.
Show User Shortcuts
page will show shortcuts to items used commonly in day-to-day use, including editing users, permission groups, automated schedules and calendars.
Show Guided Tour Shortcuts
page will show shortcuts to guided tours that will guide the user through various tasks in the system.
Note: The specific guided tours will not be displayed if the user does not have permission to the task the tour is demonstrating.
Show Status Monitoring Shortcuts
page will show shortcuts to view/control doors, areas, outputs, inputs and events.
Note: If the user’s web profile does not include the ability to view or control one of these items, the respective shortcuts will not appear on the

page shortcuts for the control of areas, doors and outputs with the click and tap control. Once a door, area or output is added, they will appear on the
page as they do in the state/control page. The user will be able to directly control the item by simply clicking the item.
Note: The control of these items are based on the user’s permissions. For a user to control an output, it must be configured as a user controllable output.

Elite LCD Terminal
Elite LCD Terminals
Logon Security
Sets the maximum number of illegal PIN attempts before LCD Terminal will lock for the
Lockout Time
The system will communicate the
Max PIN Attempts
event to a monitoring station if alarm reporting is enabled and correctly configured.
Determines the duration of time that the keypad will be unresponsive if a user exceeds the
Keypad Beeper
If enabled, the keypad beeper will sound an audible tone with each key press.
Always Enable Backlight

If enabled, ensures that the LCD Terminal backlight is always enabled, even when logged off. This may be useful if the terminal is mounted in a dark area to have attention drawn to it or to be able to see the logged off display text.

By default, the backlight on the terminal will be off when logged off, but will turn on when interacted with or displaying a message about the associated area or door state. The backlight can also be turned on briefly by pressing a button that does not have a function when logged off, such as the ON, OFF, MENU or an Arrow key.

Logged Off Behaviour
Determines what is displayed on the LCD Terminal when logged off.

Alarm Message Sources

Determines whether or not area alarm messages are displayed on the LCD Terminal when logged off. Will display the last alarm message that occurred in an area, including the input that triggered the alarm. Once someone has seen the message the user can press the
[END]
key or log on to the terminal to clear the message. This will also clear the message from all other terminals.
- Area alarm messages are not displayed on the Logged Off screen, this is useful in situations where alarms must be discreetly generated or would rather be ignored in certain locations.
- Alarm messages from all areas are displayed on this LCD Terminal's logged off screen.
Note, if an area is associated to the terminal via the
option, the
Area Alarm Active
feedback of the terminal will override the alarm messages from that area
If enabled, outputs can be controlled by a user from the logged off screen. Outputs must be configured as
and have the
Logged Off Control
option enabled, see the
page. Press the
button (up arrow) on the LCD Terminal to access output control.
If enabled, a duress event will be sent to the monitoring station when a user presses the
button on the LCD Terminal three times in a row.

Note, careful consideration should be given when enabling this option if this terminal is located in a public place, as a silent alarm could be easily activated by someone playing with the terminal who is unfamiliar with the system
Module Number
This number is the unique module address and is used to distinguish between modules when more than one type of module is connected to Inception.

When adding a new module to inception the module is addressed based on the provided instruction manual. If a module is connected to the Inception Controller LAN it will be auto discovered and will indicate the module number, the module number field will not be editable.

If a module is added to the Inception Controller by clicking the Add New button on the hardware configuration page you are able to edit and select a module number, ensure that the physical module is addressed the same module number when connecting it later.

The maximum duration of time that the module can remain out of communication with the controller. If the module does not communicate within the poll time, it is considered to be offline and will create a hardware event.
The system will communicate this hardware event to a monitoring station if alarm reporting is enabled and correctly configured.

Integrated Connections
Basic Connection Details
Device Name
The Name of device being connected to.
Software Version
The Software Version of device being connected to.
Hardware Version
The Hardware Version of device being connected to.
Manufacturer ID
The Manufacturer ID of device being connected to.
The Serial Number of device being connected to.
Connected Devices Count
The Number of devices or cameras connected to Integrated connected device
Basic Details
Parent ID
Connection Device Type
The type of device being connected to.
Block Untrusted Certificates

If enabled, this Integrated Connection will fail to validate the remote certificate of the integration if is not trusted, such as in scenarios with a self-signed certificate or improper certificate installation.
Block Invalid Certificates

If enabled, this Integrated Connection will fail to validate the remote certificate of the integration if the certificate is invalid in any way, such as when the certificate is misconfigured, the domain is mismatched, or there is a break in the certificate's chain of trust.
Trusted Certificate Fingerprint

This value is the currently trusted certificate fingerprint for this Integrated Connection. If this value matches the one found while attempting to validate the certificate of the Integrated Connection, it will be successfully validated given that there are no other errors and it has not been blocked due to the Integrated Connection's configuration.

The first successful connection to the integration will automatically trust the certificate fingerprint of that connection. After that, this value must be changed manually or matched to the last attempted connection's fingerprint using the link icon to the left.
Connection Door Type
The type of door being connected to.

8 Output Expanders

8 Input Expanders
Standard LAN Access Module (SLAM)
Standard LAN Access Modules (SLAMs)
IR-Paradym RF Module
IR-Paradym RF Modules
RF Device Supervision
Enable RF Poll Supervision
If enabled, the RF inputs configured for this module will be monitored to ensure they regularly poll in. If an input fails to poll in within the specified time (configured with the
RF Poll Supervision Time
option below), an RF Poll event will be sent to a monitoring station if alarm reporting is enabled. Notifications to users can also be sent in these cases.
Note that inputs that fail to poll in will not causes area alarms or sirens to occur, however they will cause area arm attempts to fail if an input seal check is performed.
If RF poll supervision is enabled, this option determines the amount of time that is allowed to pass before an input is considered to have failed to poll in.

The amount of time to select should depend on the poll frequency of your devices. It is recommended that the selected time is at least two or three times longer than the longest device poll time. As polls can occasionally be missed, this should help to prevent unnecessary poll fail messages from being reported due to a single missed poll.
PIN Login Required
Enable this to require the PIN number to be entered immediately on waking the terminal.

Otherwise all users are required to enter a PIN at least once per active Touchscreen session on interacting with Areas, Input, Outputs or Doors. If disarming an area in Exit delay the user will be required to enter the PIN.

This option can be used to prevent unauthorized users interacting with the touchscreen. This feature is generally enabled where the touchscreen is accessible to the public and ensures any touchscreen operation and system status/s remain secure. For private dwellings such as residential homes, this feature is generally not enabled.
PINs can be created and updated on the Manage Users on the Inception webpage and Users will require related Permissions and LCD Terminal Profile to properly interact with the Touchscreen device.
PIN Required for Emergency
Enable this option to require that a PIN number must be provided at some point to access the Emergency request options.
Note that this option is only relevant if the above

option is disabled, to support a scenario where a User can interact with the Touchscreen without providing a PIN to wake it up, but access to the emergency shortcuts still requires a PIN code. This ability may be desired in home environments where interacting with the touchscreen without a PIN lets the user see the system status easily, but they still want to prevent someone from accidentally requesting emergency services.
Sets the maximum number of illegal PIN attempts before the touchscreen will lock for the
12V Power Monitoring
If enabled, the touchscreen will monitor the 12V input for Low Voltage and will be included in the overall system health monitoring.
Note: Only enable this option if this module is connected to a 12V Power Supply and not a dedicated PoE connection.
The Inception Controller will report this hardware event to a monitoring station if alarm reporting is enabled and correctly configured, should any alarm conditions occur with this power supply.
